subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the theme and message of the poem 'Somebody’s Mother'
Analyse the characters and their actions in the poem
Understand the importance of empathy and social responsibility
Identify poetic devices used in the poem
Develop critical thinking and reading comprehension skills

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Empathy and Social Responsibility
student_activities: Discuss situations where people need help

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Understanding the Poem
teaching_strategy: Discussion on imagery in the poem

lesson_plan.title: Conflict and Character Behaviour
topics_covered: Behaviour of the group of boys
student_activities: Compare the behaviour of the boys and the young helper
Discuss why people sometimes ignore others in need

lesson_plan.title: Turning Point in the Poem
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the turning point
student_activities: Role-play the scene of helping the old woman

lesson_plan.title: Theme and Message of the Poem
topics_covered: Social values reflected in the poem
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on the poem’s message
student_activities: Write a short paragraph on the poem’s message

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyse why only one boy helped the woman. This discussion helps students understand empathy, moral responsibility, and how individual actions can influence society.
